title: Correction: Preventing lead leakage in perovskite solar cells and modules with a low-cost and stable chemisorption coating
abstract: Correction for ‘Preventing lead leakage in perovskite solar cells and modules with a low-cost and stable chemisorption coating’ by Zongxu Zhang et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2024, 11 , 2449–2456, https://doi.org/10.1039/D4MH00033A.

title: Corrosion response of steels fabricated through arc directed energy deposition additive manufacturing: a review
abstract: The review examines corrosion responses of WAAM steels, summarizing common production techniques, discussing both uniform and localized corrosion types, proposing corrosion mechanisms for WAAM steel, and offering suggestions for future research.

title: Dynamic metal–ligand coordination enables a hydrogel with rewritable dual-mode pattern display
abstract: Dual-mode hydrogels with structural and fluorescent colors are made of rigid pDGI and flexible PAAm/PAAc-CDs. Al 3+ binding shifts the structural color and the fluorescence simultaneously, which can be restored.

title: Entropy engineering enhances the electromagnetic wave absorption of high-entropy transition metal dichalcogenides/N-doped carbon nanofiber composites
abstract: Entropy engineering, component engineering and distinctive hierarchical structure synergize to promote the electromagnetic wave absorption of high-entropy transition metal dichalcogenide/N-doped carbon nanofiber composites.

title: Effect of Sc spatial distribution on the electronic and ferroelectric properties of AlScN
abstract: Effect of Sc spatial distribution on the ferroelectric properties of AlScN.

title: Polyethylene glycol-decorated n-type conducting polymers with improved ion accessibility for high-performance organic electrochemical transistors
abstract: Optimized polyethylene glycol decoration of n-type conducting polymer PBFDO enabled well-balanced electron transport and ion accessibility, boosting the performance of organic electrochemical transistors (OECTs) and complementary circuits.

title: Design principle of disordered rocksalt type overlithiated anode for high energy density batteries
abstract: Li 3+ x M 2 O 5 with M being V, Cr, Nb, Mn, and Fe are identified among 23 potential redox centers as promising overlithiated DRX anodes with high energy density, good stability, good electronic conductivity, small volumetric change, and low cost.

title: Unveiling the role of linear alkyl organic cations in 2D layered tin halide perovskite field-effect transistors
abstract: 2D tin halide perovskites are promising semiconductors for FETs owing to their fascinating electronic properties. The charge transport properties of 2D tin halide perovskites are systematically investigated using linear alkyl ammonium cations with different carbon chain lengths.

title: Are topological insulators promising thermoelectrics?
abstract: Simply put, yes. Band inversion-driven warping makes topological insulators fascinating prospects for thermoelectric applications.

title: Unraveling nanosprings: morphology control and mechanical characterization
abstract: Recent evaluations of nanospring synthesis methods, mechanical properties, simulations, and applications are analyzed. Future perspectives focus on molecular engineering, advanced synthesis & characterizations, and machine learning.

title: Highly elastic relaxor ferroelectrics for wearable energy storage
abstract: The first report using peroxide cross-linking to create intrinsic high-elastic relaxor ferroelectric materials for elastic energy storage.

title: Reticular chemistry-aided effective design of new second-order nonlinear optical selenites
abstract: Drawing on the ideas of reticular chemistry, seven novel NCS selenites, AM 3 [SeO 3 ] 2 [Se 2 O 5 ] 3 (A = K + /Rb + /Cs + ; M = Al 3+ /Ga 3+ /In 3+ ), were successfully designed and synthesized using a honeycomb-layered topology as the blueprint for the target network.

title: Aluminothermic reduction of CeO 2 : mechanism of an economical route to aluminum–cerium alloys
abstract: Time-resolved X-ray diffraction enabled mechanistic insight into the aluminothermic reduction of CeO 2 . The environmentally friendly process enables a direct route to Al–Ce alloy production and a high-value use for excess Ce from rare earth mining.

title: Assembled mechanical metamaterials with integrated functionalities of programmable multistability and multitransition behaviors
abstract: We propose a systematic strategy to create multistable metamaterials with function-oriented multitransition behaviors to satisfy diverse application scenarios by the replacement of designed contact block (CB) plates.

title: Evaporation-induced self-assembly of liquid crystal biopolymers
abstract: This review encapsulates recent progress in evaporation-induced self-assembly of liquid crystal biopolymers. It introduces various anisotropic structures of biopolymers in thin film and try to figure out the formation mechanism of the structures.

title: A mechanically robust spiral fiber with ionic–electronic coupling for multimodal energy harvesting
abstract: In this work, a concept of ionic–electronic coupling fibers by integrating a 2D MXene and a polymer electrolyte to fabricate spiral metastructures is proposed to realize multimodal power generation from various sources simultaneously.

title: Treasure-bowl style bifunctional site in cerium–tungsten hetero-clusters for superior solar-driven hydrogen production
abstract: Electrochemical water splitting powered by renewable energy sources hold potential for clean hydrogen production.

title: S-vacancy-assisted fast charge transport and oriented ReS 2 growth in twin crystal Zn x Cd 1− x S: an atomic-level heterostructure for dual-functional photocatalytic conversion
abstract: An atomic-level heterostructure with ReS 2 positioning at the S vacancy in twin-crystal Zn 0.5 Cd 0.5 S solid solution (ReS 2 / Sv -ZCS) achieves the improved charge kinetic behavior and efficiency for dual-functional photocatalytic conversion.

title: High green index electromagnetic interference shields with semiconducting Bi 2 S 3 fillers in a PEDOT:PSS matrix
abstract: Conventional metallic electromagnetic interference (EMI) shields, as well as the emerging 2D material-based shields, meet the shielding effectiveness (SE) needs of most applications.

title: High defect tolerance β-CsSnI 3 perovskite light-emitting diodes
abstract: Stabilizing β-CsSnI 3 at room temperature by crystal-phase engineering provides a novel and fundamental perspective to enhance the near-infrared emission of black-phase CsSnI 3 perovskites.

title: Performance of ferrite nanoparticles in inductive heating swing adsorption (IHSA): how tailoring material properties can circumvent the design limitations of a system
abstract: Tailoring inductor material properties such as coercivity and magnetic susceptibility via chemical composition is key to maximize heating performance within the limits of real-world inductive heating swing adsorption processes.

title: High density electron doping in boron-doped twisted bilayer graphene: a ladder to extended flat-band
abstract: Realizing Von Hove singularity (VHS) and extended flat bands of graphene near the Fermi level ( E F ) to explore many-body interactions with a high tendency towards superconductivity.

title: Mixed anion control of enhanced negative thermal expansion in the oxysulfide of PbTiO 3
abstract: Anion-mediated enhanced NTE in PbTiO 3 -based perovskites was first reported in the present study, which provides a new way for the design of high-performance NTE materials.

title: Diagnosis of high-Ni NCA/Gr-Si cells before rapid capacity drop by monitoring the heterogeneous degradation
abstract: Understanding the degradation of lithium-ion batteries is of utmost significance for preventing unexpected capacity drops and addressing safety concerns.

title: A reflection on ‘Highly dispersible polypyrrole nanospheres for advanced nanocomposite ultrafiltration membranes’
abstract: Lin and Kaner reflect on their group’;s Materials Horizons paper (Liao, Li and Kaner et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2014, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1039/C3MH00049D) published in the journal and discuss how their work may have influenced the research field.

title: Correction: Solution-processed white OLEDs with power efficiency over 90 lm W −1 by triplet exciton management with a high triplet energy level interfacial exciplex host and a high reverse intersystem crossing rate blue TADF emitter
abstract: Correction for ‘Solution-processed white OLEDs with power efficiency over 90 lm W −1 by triplet exciton management with a high triplet energy level interfacial exciplex host and a high reverse intersystem crossing rate blue TADF emitter’ by Liang Chen et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2022, 9 , 1299–1308, ht…

title: Realizing long-term cycling stability of O3-type layered oxide cathodes for sodium-ion batteries
abstract: The synergistic regulation of the electronic structure based on Li/Sn co-substitution effectively activates more Ni 2+/3+ and Fe 3+/4+ redox reactions in the high-voltage area and suppresses the redox reactivity of Mn 3+/4+ below 2.5 V.

title: A hydrogen radical pathway for efficacious electrochemical nitrate reduction to ammonia over an Fe-polyoxometalate/Cu electrocatalyst
abstract: We report a hydrogen radical (H*) pathway over Fe-POM/Cu, observed via operando Fe (H* generation) and Cu (NO 3 − binding) redoxes giving high faradaic efficiency (up to ~97.09%) for NO 3 − reduction to NH 3 with a ~36-fold higher yield than pristine Cu.

title: Kirkendall effect induced ultrafine VOOH nanoparticles and their transformation into VO 2 (M) for energy-efficient smart windows
abstract: Ultrafine VOOH nanoparticles were prepared using the Kirkendall effect, and the VOOH can be transformed to VO 2 with advanced thermochromic properties.

title: Clay–polymer hybrid hydrogels in the vanguard of technological innovations for bioremediation, metal biorecovery, and diverse applications
abstract: Hybrid clay/polymer composite hydrogels immobilized with microbial cells offer a novel, green, and effective methodology for bioremediation, biorecovery, and other applications, due to their enhanced properties and functionalities.

title: Multifunctional strain-activated liquid–metal composite films with electromechanical decoupling for stretchable electromagnetic shielding
abstract: Stretchable liquid metal composite films with strain-insensitive electromagnetic interference shielding performance are fabricated via scraping and pre-stretching. These films also have potential applications in Joule heating and motion monitoring.

title: Correction: High-performance one-dimensional halide perovskite crossbar memristors and synapses for neuromorphic computing
abstract: Correction for ‘High-performance one-dimensional halide perovskite crossbar memristors and synapses for neuromorphic computing’ by Sujaya Kumar Vishwanath et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2024, 11 , 2643–2656, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3MH02055J.

title: Skin-adhesive and self-healing diagnostic wound dressings for diabetic wound healing recording and electrophysiological signal monitoring
abstract: A diagnostic wound dressing was fabricated, which could record the diabetic wounds healing and monitor electrophysiological signals. The flexible substrate of dressings shows excellent tissue adhesive, self-healing, and antibacterial properties.

title: 3D grape string-like heterostructures enable high-efficiency sodium ion capture in Ti 3 C 2 T x MXene/fungi-derived carbon nanoribbon hybrids
abstract: A 3D grape string-like heterostructure of Ti 3 C 2 T x hollow microspheres/fungi-derived N-doping carbon nanoribbons was prepared through template method and bio-adsorption, and its HCDI performance was discussed.

title: Strong and anti-swelling nanofibrous hydrogel composites inspired by biological tissue for amphibious motion sensors
abstract: Nanofibrous hydrogel composites with exceptional mechanical, electrical, and anti-swelling properties, enabling amphibious motion sensing, underwater communication, and biological monitoring.

title: Ultramicroporous crosslinked polyxanthene-poly(biphenyl piperidinium)-based anion exchange membranes for water electrolyzers operating under highly alkaline conditions
abstract: The ultramicroporous crosslinked polyxanthene-poly(biphenyl piperidinium)-based anion exchange membrane water electrolyzer demonstrated exceptional electrolysis performance under highly alkaline conditions.

title: A high-performance organic lithium salt-doped OFET with the optical radical effect for photoelectric pulse synaptic simulation and neuromorphic memory learning
abstract: Spin-coating-derived OFETs for photoelectric pulse synaptic simulation have been actualized. The application of EDL and the introduction of Cl-HABI play a crucial role in the construction and implementation of the neural morphology system.

title: Glass-like thermal conductivity and phonon transport mechanism in disordered crystals
abstract: Glass-like thermal conductivity ( κ ) in disordered crystals was characterized successfully by multimodal phonon transport. Reducing κ via high-entropy engineering may be limited due to the differences in interatomic bonding.

title: Ferroelectric, flexoelectric and photothermal coupling in PVDF-based composites for flexible photoelectric sensors
abstract: Through ferroelectric, flexoelectric and photothermal coupling, the prepared flexible PVDF/BZTM–BCT:Au composite film presents an obvious photovoltaic response to visible light, showing versatility in sensing photons, deformation and movement.

title: Efficient solution-processed fluorescent OLEDs realized by removing charge trapping emission loss of BODIPY fluorochrome
abstract: Using a BDP-2 fluorochrome, an ultra-low doping strategy is suggested to develop solution-processed TADF-sensitized OLEDs without charge trapping emission loss for the first time, achieving a record EQE max. level of 21.5%.

title: Engineering a hollow bowl-like porous carbon-confined Ru–MgO hetero-structured nanopair as a high-performance catalyst for ammonia borane hydrolysis
abstract: A sub-nano interfacial catalytic structure is orchestrated by tailor-designing a hollow bowl-like porous carbon-confined Ru–MgO hetero-structured nanopair as a high-performance catalyst for ammonia borane hydrolysis.

title: Slippery hydrogel surface on PTFE hollow fiber membranes for sustainable emulsion separation
abstract: Establishing an efficient and sustainable membrane module is of great significance for practical oil/water emulsion separation.

title: Customized surface adhesive and wettability properties of conformal electronic devices
abstract: This review covers advances in conformal electronics surface engineering, focusing on wettability and adhesion to improve adaptability to wet conditions and irregular skin textures, enhancing durability and signal stability.

title: Crosslinking-induced anion transport control for enhancing linearity in organic synaptic devices
abstract: A novel approach using click chemistry-designed photocrosslinking can effectively modulate ion transport in conjugated polymers, leading to improved linearity and nonvolatility in artificial synaptic devices.

title: Hyper strength, high sensitivity integrated wearable signal sensor based on non-covalent interaction of an ionic liquid and bacterial cellulose for human behavior monitoring
abstract: Ion-sensing hydrogels exhibit electrical conductivity, softness, and mechanical and sensory properties akin to human tissue, rendering them an ideal material for mimicking human skin.

title: Revealing the full potential of CsPbIBr 2 perovskite solar cells: advancements towards enhanced performance
abstract: Cesium lead iodide bromide (CsPbIBr 2 ) perovskite solar cells (PSCs) have improved stability compared to other perovskite compositions.

title: In situ self-reconstructed hierarchical bimetallic oxyhydroxide nanosheets of metallic sulfides for high-efficiency electrochemical water splitting
abstract: The obtained bimetallic sulfide catalyst can be reconstituted as FeCoOOH, which has high efficacy for water splitting. The activation energy barrier of key reaction steps can be effectively reduced by dual-metal cooperation.

title: Leveraging volatile memristors in neuromorphic computing: from materials to system implementation
abstract: This review explores various mechanisms enabling threshold switching in volatile memristors and introduces recent progress in the implementation of neuromorphic computing systems based on these mechanisms.

title: High-toughness, extensile and self-healing PDMS elastomers constructed by decuple hydrogen bonding
abstract: Elastomers are widely used in traditional industries and new intelligent fields.

title: A next-generation transistor with low supply voltage operation constructed based on 2D materials' metal–semiconductor phase transition
abstract: Power dissipation, a fundamental limitation for realizing high-performance electronic devices, may be effectively reduced by an external supply voltage.

title: Facile strategy for intrinsic low- κ dielectric polymers: molecular design based on space charge conservation
abstract: We propose an innovative strategy that utilizes the space charge distribution characteristics of the co-monomer to solve the trade-off between optimizing the dielectric performance of polymers and maintaining the other comprehensive properties.

title: Visible-light-excited organic room temperature phosphorescence
abstract: This review summarizes great advancements in visible-light-excited RTP, mainly focusing on the construction strategies, optical properties, underlying emission mechanisms, and innovative applications.

title: Direct restoration of photocurable cross-linkers for repeated light-based 3D printing of covalent adaptable networks
abstract: Photocurable dynamic cross-linkers based on β-amino esters have been successfully synthesized. The direct restoration of such cross-linkers, enabled by a catalyst- and solvent-free protocol, is established for repeated DLP 3D printing.

title: Injectable hydrogels for bone regeneration with tunable degradability via peptide chirality modification
abstract: A matrix metalloproteinase (MMP)-responsive degradable hydrogel crosslinked with l -chiral MMP-sensitive peptides exhibits an enhanced degradation rate, contributing to promoted cell migration for effective bone regeneration.

title: Origin and suppression of dark current for high-performance colloidal quantum dot short-wave infrared photodetectors
abstract: This review discusses the dark current generation mechanisms and summarized the main strategies for suppressing dark current in colloidal quantum dot short-wave photodiodes. Conclusions and perspectives are provided as a guideline for this field.

title: Tunable sub-terahertz resonance absorption in high-coercivity magnetodielectric ceramics
abstract: Dense ceramics of Al-substituted hexaferrites demonstrate robust, intensive, and highly magnetic field-sensitive ferromagnetic resonance absorption at 5–300 K.

title: Ferrovalleytricity in a two-dimensional antiferromagnetic lattice
abstract: Through employing Zeeman fields, we present a general mechanism for extending the ferrovalleytricity paradigm to antiferromagnetic lattices to achieve spin control over valley physics.

title: An injectable and degradable heterogeneous microgel assembly capable of forming a “micro-nest group” for cell condensation and cartilage regeneration
abstract: The microgel assembly (F/S-MA) can be injected into a defect site and spontaneously form a “micro-nest group” in situ to guide cell homing, condensation, and chondrogenic differentiation.

title: A polyamino acid-based phosphatidyl polymer library for in vivo mRNA delivery with spleen targeting ability
abstract: A novel polyamino acid-based phosphatidyl polymer library, which combines the advantages of phospholipids in cellular uptake and polymers in stabilization, can effectively deliver mRNA to the spleen following systemic administration.

title: Dual access to the fluid networks of colloid-stabilized bicontinuous emulsions through uninterrupted connections
abstract: Bijels intertwine two fluids into stable, bicontinuous networks. This work introduces dual pore connections for bijels to two bulk phases, a unique architecture with potentials to overcome diffusion limitations for batteries and separation membranes.

title: Dual-targeted fluorescent probe for tracking polarity and phase transition processes during lipophagy
abstract: The multifunctional fluorescent probe PTZ enables investigation of the LDs and lysosome interaction from both phase transition and polarity perspectives, and the homogeneous transition of LDs and lysosomes during lipophagy was discovered.

title: Designing a photocatalytic and self-renewed g-C 3 N 4 nanosheet/poly-Schiff base composite coating towards long-term biofouling resistance
abstract: A new long-term anti-biofouling composite coating combining g-C 3 N 4 nanosheets with degradable green poly-Schiff base resins, which integrates the dual functions of enhanced dynamic self-renewal and photocatalytic antibacterial activities.

title: ROS-responsive hydrogels: from design and additive manufacturing to biomedical applications
abstract: Hydrogels with intricate 3D networks and high hydrophilicity have qualities resembling those of biological tissues, making them ideal candidates for use as smart biomedical materials.

title: Succulent inspired grown g-C 3 N 4 @lithium sodium niobate for supercapacitors and piezo-tuned electrochemical potential controlled smart electromagnetic shielding management
abstract: This study illustrates the synthesis and application of succulent-inspired grown GN@LNN for self-charging supercapacitors and real-time EMI shielding manipulations.

title: High-performance sono-piezoelectric nanocomposites enhanced by interfacial coupling effects for implantable nanogenerators and actuators
abstract: High-performance implantable and wireless P(VDF-CTFE)/BT@Carbon piezoelectric nanogenerators are developed for harvesting ultrasound for biological device power supply.

title: C 3 N 5 -based nanomaterials and their applications in heterogeneous catalysts, energy harvesting, and environmental remediation
abstract: The semiconductor material C 3 N 5 , due to its unique physicochemical properties and excellent material performance, has been widely researched and applied in the fields of energy, catalysis and environmental remediation.

title: Recent progress in self-healable energy harvesting and storage devices – a future direction for reliable and safe electronics
abstract: This review summarizes the advances in self-healing materials developed for electrodes and electrolytes of energy harvesting and storage devices (TENG, supercapacitors and batteries) with enhanced durability and functionality.

title: A reflection on ‘The synthesis, structure and electronic properties of a lead-free hybrid inorganic–organic double perovskite (MA) 2 KBiCl 6 (MA = methylammonium)’
abstract: Cheetham et al. reflect on one of their first Materials Horizons papers ( Mater. Horiz. , 2016, https://doi.org/10.1039/C6MH00053C) published in the journal and discuss how their work may have influenced the research field.

title: A structural color hydrogel for diagnosis of halitosis and screening of periodontitis
abstract: A structural color hydrogel for naked-eye detection of exhaled VSCs is developed, providing an approach for low-cost, high-sensitivity, and high-accuracy point-of-care diagnosis of halitosis and screening of periodontitis.

title: Thermomechanically stable supramolecular elastomers inspired by heat shock proteins
abstract: Inspired by heat shock proteins, we show the thermomechanically stable supramolecular elastomer through heat-induced reversible transformation of boron-based dynamic interactions.

title: Neuromorphic devices realised using self-forming hierarchical Al and Ag nanostructures: towards energy-efficient and wide ranging synaptic plasticity
abstract: Self-formed hierarchical structures of Al and Ag closely mimicking the biological neural network offer wide range synaptic plasticity with ultra-low energy usage. Al islands can be exploited as contact pads to introduce multiple sensory signals.

title: Alternating current electroluminescence devices: recent advances and functional applications
abstract: This review deeply explores the application of alternating current electroluminescence in functional and visualization sensing, particularly emphasizing its future potential in human–machine visual interaction.

title: Oxygen tracer diffusion in amorphous hafnia films for resistive memory
abstract: We quantified the oxygen tracer diffusion in amorphous hafnium oxide thin films. These tracer diffusion values are consistent with the experimentally measured retention times of hafnium oxide resistive memory devices.

title: The afterglow of carbon dots shining in inorganic matrices
abstract: This is a comprehensive and systematic review focusing on CDs@IMs from multiple perspectives of construction, performance, mechanism, and multifunctional applications.

title: High-entropy materials for thermoelectric applications: towards performance and reliability
abstract: This review summarizes the recent progress in the design of high-entropy thermoelectric materials, including alloys and ceramics, and emphasises the entropy-driven effect in these thermoelectric materials.

title: Mechanical metamaterials as broadband electromagnetic wave absorbers: investigating relationships between geometrical parameters and electromagnetic response
abstract: This work explores the correlations with geometrical parameters (cell length, volume fraction, multilayer) and electromagnetic response of lightweight and load-bearing mechanical metamaterials when used as a broadband absorber.

title: Unlocking the potential of self-healing and recyclable ionic elastomers for soft robotics applications
abstract: Mechanically strong, self-healing and recyclable rubber-based ionic elastomers for soft robotics hand.

title: Correction: Thermoelectric nanowires for dense 3D printed architectures
abstract: Correction for ‘Thermoelectric nanowires for dense 3D printed architectures’ by Danwei Zhang et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2024, 11 , 847–854, https://doi.org/10.1039/D3MH01646C.

title: Engineering hierarchical interfaces in high-temperature polymer dielectrics for electrostatic supercapacitors
abstract: Heterojunction interface engineering effectively increases energy levels and reduces conduction losses, achieving ultrahigh discharged energy density and charge–discharge efficiency over a broad temperature range.

title: Synaptic memristors based on BaTiO 3 thin films irradiated by swift heavy ions for neuromorphic computing
abstract: We report the artificial synaptic plasticity of BTO-based memristors and its application to artificial neural network computation by using swift heavy-ion irradiation as a tool for tuning the defects in the BTO layer.

title: Thermo-growing ion clusters enabled healing strengthening and tough adhesion for highly reliable skin electronics
abstract: A thermo-growing ion cluster-based elastomer was developed to achieve healing strengthening and tough adhesion features.

title: Designed metal–organic π-clusters combining the aromaticity of the metal cluster and ligands for a third-order nonlinear optical response
abstract: “Metal–organic π-clusters” were proposed, combining the π-electrons of both the cluster core and ligand for significant applications in third-order nonlinear optical materials.

title: Excellent thermomagnetic power generation for harvesting waste heat via a second-order ferromagnetic transition
abstract: NiMnIn Heusler alloys with second-order ferromagnetic transition show good thermomagnetic generation (TMG) performance with zero hysteresis and a long-term service life, enabling them to be better candidates for practical applications of TMG.

title: Cold-resistant, highly stretchable ionic conductive hydrogels for intelligent motion recognition in winter sports
abstract: Conductive hydrogels have attracted much attention for their wide application in the field of flexible wearable sensors due to their outstanding flexibility, conductivity and sensing properties.

title: Antimicrobial zeolites and metal–organic frameworks
abstract: The literature on antimicrobial applications of zeolites and metal–organic frameworks is reviewed with a focus on analytical techniques, antimicrobial mechanisms, and functional materials design.

title: Construction of antibacterial bone implants and their application in bone regeneration
abstract: In order to address the issue of bacterial infection during the transplantation of bone implants, four types of bone implants with long-term antimicrobial functionality have been constructed.

title: Photo-controllable azobenzene microdroplets on an open surface and their application as transporters
abstract: Photoresponsive microdroplets exhibit directional and continuous movement on a glass surface when simultaneously irradiated with two different light sources. Additionally, these droplets function as carriers for fluorescent quantum dots.

title: Floquet engineering of the orbital Hall effect and valleytronics in two-dimensional topological magnets
abstract: Multiple topological phase transitions with a tunable orbital Hall effect under the irradiation of right-handed or left-handed circularly polarized light in two-dimensional ferromagnets.

title: A bioactive calcium silicate nanowire-containing hydrogel for organoid formation and functionalization
abstract: An optimized CS/GelMA composite hydrogel is designed for organoid development, which can be used as a substrate for supporting intestinal and liver organoid formation and functionalization.

title: Programmable and resilient metamaterials with anisotropic and non-linear mechanical responses composed exclusively of stiff constituents
abstract: We propose a systematic strategy to create a family of three-dimensional programmable resilient mechanical metamaterials with robust resilience and anisotropic non-linear response, without relying on flexible or hyperelastic components.

title: Full automation of point defect detection in transition metal dichalcogenides through a dual mode deep learning algorithm
abstract: We advocate for the development of expertise in visualizing and identifying point defects in two-dimensional (2D) materials, a skillset intimately linked to a wide array of physical phenomena.

title: Thermally activated delayed fluorescence tetradentate ligand-containing gold( iii ) complexes with preferential molecular orientation and their application in organic light-emitting devices
abstract: A new class of TADF pyridine-/pyrazine-containing tetradentate C^C^N^N gold( iii ) complexes is reported. Through strategic structural modifications, the horizontal alignment of the emitters and the resulting OLED performance can be enhanced.

title: Constructing asymmetric gradient structures to enhance the energy storage performance of PEI-based composite dielectrics
abstract: The asymmetric gradient design of the polymer-based composite dielectric can inhibit carrier injection and transport simultaneously, which significantly improves the energy density at room temperature and high temperature.

title: Real-time screening of Ni x B y bifunctional electrocatalysts for overall NH 3 synthesis via SG-TC SECM
abstract: Real-time SECM screening of synthesized catalysts towards NRR-OER bifunctional activity is presented and found consistent with half-cell investigations. The as-assembled full cell produce NH 3 (1.08 mg h −1 mg −1 ) and O 2 (0.81 mg h −1 mg −1 ) as products.

title: Enhanced photocatalysis of metal/covalent organic frameworks by plasmonic nanoparticles and homo/hetero-junctions
abstract: Metal–organic frameworks (MOFs) and covalent organic frameworks (COFs) photocatalysts face challenge in poor photocatalytic efficiency, which was promoted by the introduction of plasmonic nanoparticles (NPs) and junctions.

title: Photo-induced synthesis of polymeric nanoparticles and chemiluminescent degradable materials via flow chemistry
abstract: Polymeric nanospheres can be synthesised within minutes using photo-flow chemistry, without the need for initiators, additives, or surfactants. The particles can be selectively degraded on demand, emitting light in the process.

title: Multicolor vision perception of flexible optoelectronic synapse with high sensitivity for skin sunburn warning
abstract: We propose a self-powered flexible optoelectronic synapse based on PEA 2 SnI 4 films for multicolor vision perception and skin sunburn warning.

title: Sulfur-regulated CoSe 2 nanowires with high-charge active centers for electrochemical nitrate reduction to ammonium
abstract: The structural evolution of CoSe 2 from 2D to 1D is achieved through the precise regulation of sulfur. The obtained S-CoSe 2 NWs can efficiently mobilize nitrate reduction to ammonia in temperate environments.

title: Robust myco-composites: a biocomposite platform for versatile hybrid-living materials
abstract: We report the development of strong and robust myco-composites compatible with additive manufacturing. We exemplify unique applications of this hybrid-living materials platform with fabrication of bio-welded containers and flexible mycelium textiles.

title: A high-dimensional in-sensor reservoir computing system with optoelectronic memristors for high-performance neuromorphic machine vision
abstract: A high-dimensional in-sensor reservoir computing system with optoelectronic memristors is demonstrated utilizing optical and electrical masks. Handwritten digit classification and human action recognition are successfully achieved with high accuracy.

title: Heterostructured grafting of NiFe-layered double hydroxide@TiO 2 for boosting photoelectrochemical cathodic protection
abstract: Grafting NiFe-LDH nanosheets onto TiO 2 nanorod arrays largely improves the photoexcited charge separation and accelerates the surface oxidation reaction, leading to stable & self-enhancing photoelectrochemical cathodic protection performance.

title: Design of PDMS/PAN composite membranes with ultra-interfacial stability via layer integration
abstract: Ultra-interfacial stable composite membranes based on a facile photo-triggered co-grafting PDMS layer achieve superior separation performance and prolonged lifetimes.

title: High-yield and chirality-selective isolation of single-walled carbon nanotubes using conjugated polymers and small molecular chaperones
abstract: We show a highly effective isolation of monochiral single-walled carbon nanotubes using conjugated polymers and small molecular chaperones that facilitate polymer folding.

title: Spirobifluorene-fused strategy enables pure-green multiple resonance emitters with low efficiency roll-off
abstract: The spiroannulation of multiple resonance core skeleton gives rise to pure-green emitters and superior electroluminescence performance with low efficiency roll-off.

title: Magnetic-assisted alignment of nanofibers in a polymer nanocomposite for high-temperature capacitive energy storage applications
abstract: A magnetic-assisted approach is proposed for fabricating anisotropic polymer nanocomposite with aligned nanofibers, showcasing excellent high-temperature energy storage performance.

title: Exploring the potential of all-aqueous immiscible systems for preparing complex biomaterials and cellular constructs
abstract: This review focuses on strategies to fabricate biomaterials, including the ones with higher degree complexity and cell-rich constructs, using all-aqueous immiscible systems and critically discusses their potential, key requirements, and challenges.

title: Self-limiting material growth triggered and tunable by force through piezocharge-induced mineralization
abstract: We report a self-limiting growth mechanism controllable by force based on an analytical model that is verfied experimentally using a piezoelectric substrate immersed in an electrolyte solution under cyclic loading.

title: Understanding radiation-generated electronic traps in radiation dosimeters based on organic field-effect transistors
abstract: An investigation into the origins of radiation-generated traps utilizing characterization of large area transistor arrays, spectral analysis of the trap density of states, photoluminescence spectroscopy, and GIWAXS.

title: Touch initiated on-demand adhesion on rough surfaces
abstract: Inspired by the adhesive capabilities of snails, this work successfully develops a touch-initiated hydrogel adhesive that exhibits remarkable on-demand adhesion on a diverse range of surfaces.

title: Experimental demonstration of combination-encoding content-addressable memory of 0.75 bits per switch utilizing Hf–Zr–O ferroelectric tunnel junctions
abstract: We have fabricated 4-CECAM (with keys encoded into 8-long binary arrays and 8 FTJs) with a content density of 0.75 bits per switch, which highlights 50% increase in content density compared to that of the conventional TCAM (0.5 bits per switch).

title: Scalable co-cured polyimide/poly( p -phenylene benzobisoxazole) all-organic composites enabling improved energy storage density, low leakage current and long-term cycling stability
abstract: The all-organic high-temperature polymer dielectrics with promising scale-up potential have witnessed much progress in the energy storage area, etc.

title: Unlock flow-type reversible aqueous Zn–CO 2 batteries
abstract: A flow-type reversible aqueous Zn–CO 2 battery using a Pd/SnO 2 @C cathode catalyst has been assembled and demonstrates an ultra-high power density.

title: Deep generative spatiotemporal learning for integrating fracture mechanics in composite materials: inverse design, discovery, and optimization
abstract: We introduce an advanced multi-task deep learning framework, designed to predict the evolution of stress fields and the crack propagation across both time and space.

title: IR regulation through preferential placement of h-BN nanosheets in a polymer network liquid crystal
abstract: A generic strategy of employing IR-transparent components to achieve prominent IR regulation via preferential placement of h-BN nanosheets in a polymer network liquid crystal with electric tunability.

title: Covalent organic frameworks with flexible side chains in hybrid PEMs enable highly efficient proton conductivity
abstract: Electrochemical hydrogen compression (EHC) is an emerging energy conversion technology.

title: van der Waals-bonded graphene clusters enhance thermal conductivity of phase-change materials for advanced thermal energy management
abstract: Through highly oriented arrangement of graphene, ultra-low junction thermal resistance structure, and phase-change microcapsule technology, we developed composites with high thermal conductivity (103 W m −1 K −1 ) and fast phase-change heat absorption.

title: Versatile Pickering emulsion gel lubricants stabilized by cooperative interfacial graphene oxide-polymer assemblies
abstract: A new-type Pickering emulsion gel capable of providing high colloidal stability, potent in-air and under-water lubrication, cooling capacity, and anti-corrosion and anti-swelling behavior is reported. It can also be 3D-printed into various shapes.

title: Embedded 3D printing of UV-curable thermosetting composites with continuous fiber
abstract: A new embedded 3D printing method is developed that enables the printing of high-quality continuous fiber composites with variable fiber volume fractions, matrix materials, and composite structures with large-hollow features.

title: 3D nitrogen-doped carbon frameworks with hierarchical pores and graphitic carbon channels for high-performance hybrid energy storages
abstract: 3D nitrogen-doped hierarchical porous graphitic carbon cathode and subnanometric tin oxide nanocrystals anode materials are derived from Co–Zn mixed metal–organic frameworks to achieve high-performance Li-ion and Na-ion hybrid energy storage devices.

title: Filler effects inspired high performance polyurethane elastomer design: segment arrangement control
abstract: Inspired by filler effects, a strategy to enhance the mechanical properties of TPU elastomers by designing segments arrangement is presented.

title: Elasto-plastic effects on shape-shifting electron-beam-patterned gel-based micro-helices
abstract: Gel-based polyelectrolyte micro-rods undergo both elastic and plastic deformation that drive pH-responsive changes in shape-shifting helical properties.

title: A reflection on ‘Protein coronas suppress the hemolytic activity of hydrophilic and hydrophobic nanoparticles’
abstract: Rotello et al. reflect on their first Materials Horizons paper ( Mater. Horiz. , 2014, https://doi.org/10.1039/C3MH00075C) published in the inaugural issue of the journal, and discuss how their work may have influenced the research field over the past decade.

title: Metal dimer nanojunction-magnetic material composites for magnetic field sensing
abstract: As a magnetic field sensor, the dimer nanojunction composite has a highly sensitive Raman scattering spectrum under an external magnetic field, which is caused by a change in the nanocrystal spacing in the dimer nanojunction.

title: Kirigami-enabled stretchable laser-induced graphene heaters for wearable thermotherapy
abstract: Stretchable later-induced graphene (LIG) heaters with kirigami structures are produced via laser engraving/cutting for wearable thermotherapy. Such a heater has a rapid electrothermal response and robust performance for on-body use.

title: Recent progress in SEI engineering for boosting Li metal anodes
abstract: This review summarizes the formation mechanism, structural model, composition, in situ / operando characterization, properties, and optimization strategies of a SEI.

title: Strategies for developing layered oxide cathodes, carbon-based anodes, and electrolytes for potassium ion batteries
abstract: The progress in the development of layered metal oxide cathodes, carbon-based anodes, and electrolytes for potassium ion batteries is reviewed. Specific development suggestions, concise perspectives, and strategies are provided.

title: Eliminating trade-offs between optical scattering and mechanical durability in aerogels as outdoor passive cooling metamaterials
abstract: Passive cooling is a promising approach for reducing the large energy consumption to achieve carbon neutrality.

title: Transparent porous films with real refractive index close to unity for photonic applications
abstract: Near unity refractive index porous thin films (left) allow integration of different nanoparticles to build photonic structures, such as highly diffusive random media (center) or color converting layers with enhanced luminescence outcoupling (right).

title: High-entropy alloy screening for halide perovskites
abstract: We demonstrate the new concept of using unit cell volume coefficient of variation to approximate the enthalpic penalty of high-entropy alloy (HEA) candidates, and use it along with configurational entropy to map promising HEA halide perovskites.

title: Dual-functional thermal management textiles for dynamic temperature regulation based on ultra-stretchable spiral conductive composite yarn with 500%-strain thermal stability and durability
abstract: We propose a temperature visualization and dynamic temperature management sandwich-structure textile based on an ultra-stretchable spiral conductive composite yarn, which maintains flexibility, breathability, stretchability and excellent performance stability.

title: Thermal control of photothermal implants inspired by polar bear skin for the treatment of infected bone defects
abstract: Thermal-adaptive implant coating inspired by polar bears enhances NIR therapy efficiency, and minimizes tissue damage in infected bone defects.

title: Self-evolutionary recycling of flame-retardant polyurethane foam enabled by controllable catalytic cleavage
abstract: A controllable catalytic cracking strategy was proposed for the self-evolving recycling of flame-retardant polyurethane.

title: Partially oxidized inter-doped RuNi alloy aerogel for the hydrogen evolution reaction in both alkaline and acidic media
abstract: RuNi alloy aerogels were created using supercritical ethanol drying, with optimal heterostructure formed through partial oxidation, exhibiting robust electrocatalytic properties in acid and alkaline media.

title: Revisiting the phosphonium salt chemistry for P-doped carbon synthesis: toward high phosphorus contents and beyond the phosphate environment
abstract: Calcination of THPC–urea in metal chloride hydrate salt affords porous P/N-doped carbonaceous materials (up to 18 wt% [P]), used as oxidative dehydrogenation catalysts.

title: A supramolecular approach for converting renewable biomass into functional materials
abstract: A supramolecular approach was applied to construct functional materials from natural biomass.

title: A fluorite-structured HfO 2 /ZrO 2 /HfO 2 superlattice based self-rectifying ferroelectric tunnel junction synapse
abstract: A self-rectifying ferroelectric tunnel junction that employs a HfO 2 /ZrO 2 /HfO 2 superlattice (HZH SL) combined with Al 2 O 3 and TiO 2 layers is proposed.

title: UV absorption enhanced polydopamine coating
abstract: This article illustrates a facile approach to enhance the UV absorption of PDA coatings by incorporating thiol-heterocycle derivatives. These PDA coatings showed great promise for applications in UV protection, antibacterial and dye degradation.

title: Realizing one-step two-electron transfer of naphthalene diimides via a regional charge buffering strategy for aqueous organic redox flow batteries
abstract: A novel regional charge buffering strategy was proposed to realize the “one-step two-electron” transfer process of naphthalene diimides, stabilize the output power of the battery and improve the energy efficiency of the battery.

title: Flexible, transparent, and sustainable cellulose-based films for organic solar cell substrates
abstract: Cellulose vitrimers, wherein dynamic bonds are incorporated into the cellulose network to replace a portion of the hydrogen bonds, have proven successful as flexible substrate materials for organic solar cells, outperforming traditional petroleum-based polyethylene naphthalate.

title: Alcohol-gating femtosecond laser-induced micro/nano-structured membranes with reversible switching wettability and breathability
abstract: A reversible liquid gating membrane with the ability to regulate gas/liquid transport is critical for many fields, such as biological applications, multiphase separation, and sewerage treatment.

title: Advancements and applications of upconversion nanoparticles in wound dressings
abstract: In this review, we aim to showcase the potential and benefits of up-conversion nanoparticles (UCNPs) in advanced wound care applications.

title: 4D Printable liquid crystal elastomers with restricted nanointerfacial slippage for long-term-cyclic-stability photothermal actuation
abstract: This work presents a versatile strategy to fabricate nanointerfacial-slipping-restricted photoactuation elastomers by chemically bonding the nanofillers into a thermally actuatable liquid crystal network for long-term stable cycling photoactuation.

title: N-Type polymeric mixed conductors for all-in-one aqueous electrolyte gated photoelectrochemical transistors
abstract: An n-type organic photoelectrochemical transistor produces large and reversible current changes in response to light-intensity variations in aqueous electrolytes. A long exciton lifetime of the n-type gate ensures a high photovoltage response.

title: Recent advances in artificial neuromorphic applications based on perovskite composites
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive overview of the research progress of neuromorphic devices based on composites of perovskites and different materials. The challenges and potential directions for development are also presented.

title: Strain-driven skyrmion–bimeron switching in topological magnetic monolayer CrSeBr
abstract: Skyrmion–bimeron switching is one of the most important phenomena in topological magnetism.

title: Transforming patterned defects into dynamic poly-regional topographies in liquid crystal oligomers
abstract: We transform patterned defects into high-aspect-ratio poly-regional surface topographies. The active intricate textures are induced by generated shear forces under external stimuli.

title: Atomic-scale Ru anchored on chromium-shavings as a precursor for a pH-universal hydrogen evolution reaction electrocatalyst
abstract: Chrome shavings produce electrocatalysts with atomically dispersed Ru sites. The CN/Cr 2 O 3 /Ru-1 catalyst has excellent HER catalytic performance under the synergistic effect of RuN 4 and Cr 2 O 3 .

title: Adaptive nanotube networks enabling omnidirectionally deformable electro-driven liquid crystal elastomers towards artificial muscles
abstract: Hierarchically structured electro-driven liquid crystal elastomers towards artificial muscles.

title: A polarization double-enhancement strategy to achieve super low energy consumption with ultra-high energy storage capacity in BCZT-based relaxor ferroelectrics
abstract: Due to the combination of interfacial polarization and depolarization, the energy density of BCZT-based lead-free ferroelectric reached 8.03 J cm −3 at only 425 kV cm −1 . High energy density with extremely low energy consumption was achieved.

title: A stretchable conductive elastomer sensor with self-healing and highly linear strain for human movement detection and pressure response
abstract: A self-healable conductive elastomer sensor with highly linear strain to accurately monitor real-time 3D human motion information and sense pressure.

title: Designing antiperovskite derivatives via atomic-position splitting for photovoltaic applications
abstract: Switching the A-site anion in the cubic antiperovskite structure X 3 BA from the corner to the edge center sites creates antiperovskite derivative materials .

title: Atomic scale quantum anomalous hall effect in monolayer graphene/MnBi 2 Te 4 heterostructure
abstract: The quantum anomalous Hall effect in graphene, predicted by first-principles calculations, is induced by the proximity effect of a magnetic topological insulator. Our model further reveals a rich phase diagram in graphene/MnBi 2 Te 4 heterostructures.

title: Laser-induced locally controllable craze-like microstructures for polymer white structural coloration
abstract: A simple, efficient, and locally controllable strategy for laser-induced craze-like microstructures is proposed to successfully realize white structural color on polymers added with designed CuNWs@POM core–shell microfibers.

title: Lattice doping of lanthanide ions in Cs 2 ZrCl 6 nanocrystals enabling phase transition and tunable photoluminescence
abstract: The lattice doping of lanthanide ions in Cs 2 ZrCl 6 nanocrystals enabling phase transition from a cubic phase to a monoclinic phase and tunable photoluminescence.

title: Deep-blue narrow-band emissive cesium europium bromide perovskite nanocrystals with record high emission efficiency for wide-color-gamut backlight displays
abstract: The deep-blue narrow-band emissive cesium europium bromide perovskite nanocrystals are prepared, with a record high emission efficiency of 93.51%, suitable for wide color gamut backlight displays, with a color gamut of up to 127.1% NTSC.

title: Accelerating metal nanoparticle exsolution by exploiting tolerance factor of perovskite stannate
abstract: In situ exsolution have been actively performed to deliver oxide nanocomposites with metal nanoparticles. In our work, a new ingredient was unveiled to boost nickel metal exsolution on the oxide surface by increasing the octahedron distortion.

title: Multiple neighboring active sites of an atomically precise copper nanocluster catalyst for efficient bond-forming reactions
abstract: We present a new type of core–shell copper nanocluster, [Cu 29 (S t Bu) 13 Cl 5 (PPh 3 ) 4 H 10 ] t BuSO 3 , that has multiple active sites on its surface. This nanocluster serves as a versatile heterogeneous catalyst for various C-heteroatom (C–O, C–N and C–S) bond-forming reactions.

title: A high-performance and self-powered polarization-sensitive photoelectrochemical-type Bi 2 O 2 Te photodetector based on a quasi-solid-state gel electrolyte
abstract: A quasi-solid-state photoelectrochemical-type Bi 2 O 2 Te photodetector exhibits excellent linear polarized light detection capability.

title: The dynamic nature of electrostatic disorder in organic mixed ionic and electronic conductors
abstract: The disorder in organic mixed electronic and ionic conductors (OMIECs) is highly dynamic, and, consequently, charge transport is not adversely affected by it. The dynamics of the soft materials drives the charge carriers.

title: Origin of discrete resistive switching in chemically heterogeneous vanadium oxide crystals
abstract: Vacancy-induced filament formation and rupturing across Magnéli phases of VO 2 . The resistive switching shows the variations in threshold voltage with discreteness as a function of number of cycles.

title: Phase stabilization via A-site ion anchoring for ultra-stable perovskite light emitting diodes
abstract: To enhance the operational stability of perovskite light emitting diodes (PeLEDs), 1 H ,1 H -perfluorohexylamine was employed to anchor ions and stabilize perovskite phase, yielding ambient stable film and ultra-stable PeLEDs with 37.2-year T 50 lifetime.

title: Non-Fermi liquid to charge-transfer Mott insulator in flat bands of copper-doped lead apatite
abstract: The copper-doped lead apatite, known as LK-99, is revealed as an intriguing flat-band material exhibiting correlated states from non-Fermi liquid to charge-transfer Mott insulator, rather than the initially claimed room-temperature superconductivity.

title: n-Type boron β-diketone-containing conjugated polymers for high-performance room temperature ammonia sensors
abstract: A novel strategy to modulate the charge distribution of active centers on ammonia sensing materials is reported. Boron β-diketone can effectively modulate surface electronic states, identifying the electron-deficient B ← O units as active centers.

title: Oxovanadium electronics for in-memory, neuromorphic, and quantum computing applications
abstract: Redox- and spin-active macrocyclic vanadyl( iv ) complexes and polyoxovanadates( v ) have promising structural and physicochemical characteristics for innovative bottom-up hybrid electronics.

title: Self-contact snapping metamaterial for tensile energy dissipation
abstract: We propose a mechanical metamaterial that enables energy dissipation in the tensile direction. Its mechanism, driven by elastic instability from self-contact, offers superior comprehensive performance compared to existing works.

title: New insights into pure zwitterionic hydrogels with high strength and high toughness
abstract: Highlighting the synergistic effect between liquid–liquid phase-separation and entanglement in pure polyzwitterionic hydrogels with high strength and toughness.

title: Two decades of ceria nanoparticle research: structure, properties and emerging applications
abstract: This review encompasses advances made in the development of cerium oxide nanoparticles, their structure, properties, and translation in catalysis, chemical mechanical planarization, sensing, environmental remediation and biomedicine.

title: Memristive Monte Carlo DropConnect crossbar array enabled by device and algorithm co-design
abstract: A one-selector-one-memristor crossbar array was developed, capable of driving Monte Carlo DropConnect network. This could be achieved through a hardware and algorithm co-design approach, involving mutual improvement of them.

title: Robust multiferroicity and magnetic modulation of the ferroelectric imprint field in heterostructures comprising epitaxial Hf 0.5 Zr 0.5 O 2 and Co
abstract: Magnetoelectric composite multiferroic comprising ferroelectric doped HfO 2 and ferromagnetic cobalt coupled films is demonstrated to be a promising candidate for energy efficient memory computing.

title: Advances and challenges in the modification of photoelectrode materials for photoelectrocatalytic water splitting
abstract: With the increasing consumption of fossil fuels, the development of clean and renewable alternative fuels has become a top priority.

title: Linking atomic to mesoscopic scales in multilevel structural tailoring of single-atom catalysts for peroxide activation
abstract: To enhance peroxidase-like activity, we propose a comprehensive structural refinement of single-atom catalysts, fine-tuning their electronic, geometric, and spatial configuration from the atomic to mesoscopic scales.

title: Autonomous humidity regulation by MOF/wood composites
abstract: The MOF/wood can passively regulate indoor humidity by absorbing and releasing moisture when triggered, maintaining indoor humidity at 45% relative humidity without any additional energy consumption.

title: The trade-off anionic modulation in metal–organic glasses showing color-tunable persistent luminescence
abstract: Ultralong room-temperature phosphorescence (RTP) and thermally activated delayed fluorescence (TADF) materials provide exciting opportunities for the rational design of persistent luminescence owing to their long-lived excitons.

title: Ellagic acid-modified gold nanoparticles to combat multi-drug resistant bacterial infections in vitro and in vivo
abstract: Gold nanoparticles modified with ellagic acid are prepared via a simple one-pot hydrothermal process, showing high, broad-spectrum antimicrobial activities, and effective biofilm dispersion against antibiotic-resistant ESKAPE pathogens.

title: Correction: Performance of ferrite nanoparticles in inductive heating swing adsorption (IHSA): how tailoring material properties can circumvent the design limitations of a system
abstract: Correction for ‘Performance of ferrite nanoparticles in inductive heating swing adsorption (IHSA): how tailoring material properties can circumvent the design limitations of a system’ by Maxim De Belder et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2024, 11 , 4144–4149, https://doi.org/10.1039/d4mh00377b.

title: Robust second-order topological insulator in 2D van der Waals magnet CrI 3
abstract: CrI 3 monolayer and bilayers are theoretically shown to be SOTIs with a nontrivial topology characterized by a second Stiefel–Whitney number of w 2 = 1 and the charge density distributions of corner states elegantly localized at the corners.

title: Recent progress on defect-engineering in ferroelectric HfO 2 : The next step forward via multiscale structural optimization
abstract: “Multiscale structural optimization” is assumed a next step to further optimize the unconventional ferroelectricity in HfO 2 for high-performance HfO 2 -based ferroelectrics and devices.

title: Fe/Au galvanic nanocells to generate self-sustained Fenton reactions without additives at neutral pH
abstract: Innovative Fe/Au nanogenerators are used to efficiently produce H 2 O 2 , regenerate Fe 2+ , and produce ROS at neutral pH without external additives or energy, all achieved with minimal catalyst usage and without the need for additional inputs.

title: Self-formation of compositionally complex surface oxides on high entropy alloys observed by accelerated atom probe tomography: a route to sustainable catalysts
abstract: Accelerated approach for analysis of electrochemical states with atom probe tomography reveals modification of oxygen-containing surface layers of high entropy catalysts.

title: Liquid-crystalline circularly polarised TADF emitters for high-efficiency, solution-processable organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Liquid-crystalline materials are reported that exhibit circularly polarised, thermally activated delayed fluorescence with good solution and device emission efficiency, and high luminescence asymmetry.

title: Supersensitive visual pressure sensor based on the exciton luminescence of a perovskite material
abstract: Exciton-type optical pressure sensor based on emission of the self-trapped exciton of Bi 3+ -doped double perovskite material, exhibiting giant sensitivity (112 nm GPa −1 ).

title: The interface engineering and structure design of an alloying-type metal foil anode for lithium ion batteries: a review
abstract: This review presents a comprehensive overview on alloying-type metal foil anodes toward high energy density and low-cost LIBs, calling for more attempts in the interdisciplinary fields of electrochemistry, metallic materials science, and mechanics.

title: Recent advances in flexible memristors for advanced computing and sensing
abstract: Flexible memristors enable high-performance wearable electronics with low power consumption. This review introduces the mechanisms, typical materials, and applications of flexible memristors. Potential directions and challenges are also discussed.

title: High resistance of superconducting TiN thin films against environmental attacks
abstract: Single-crystalline TiN(111) films in acid and alkaline solutions show a superior corrosion resistance compared to polycrystalline TiN films, with unexpected stability and durability of superconducticity.

title: Controlling nano- and microfilament morphology by strategically placing chiral centers in the side chains of bent-core molecules
abstract: Complex nano- and microfilament morphologies, some with rare morphological transitions, are demonstrated for a series of bent-core liquid crystal molecules, all controlled by the positioning and number of chiral centers in the aliphatic chains.

title: Dual-ligand quasi-2D perovskites with chiral-induced spin selectivity for room temperature spin-LEDs
abstract: We developed new quasi-2D perovskites with chiral and achiral organic cations as co-ligands and demonstrated their effective application in spin-LEDs at room temperature based on the chiral-induced spin selectivity (CISS) effect.

title: A responsive cascade drug delivery scaffold adapted to the therapeutic time window for peripheral nerve injury repair
abstract: Schematic illustration of the structure and drug release process of a responsive cascade drug delivery scaffold (RCDDS) for peripheral nerve injury repair.

title: Growth of emergent simple pseudo-binary ferroelectrics and their potential in neuromorphic computing devices
abstract: Ferroelectric memory devices such as ferroelectric memristors, ferroelectric tunnel junctions, and field-effect transistors are considered among the most promising candidates for neuromorphic computing devices.

title: Enhanced environmental adaptability of sandwich-like MoS 2 /Ag/WC nanomultilayer films via Ag nanoparticle diffusion-dominated defect repair
abstract: A self-healing MoS 2 /Ag/WC sandwich-like architecture utilizes spontaneous Ag atom diffusion to repair film defects and ensure long-lasting lubrication in extreme environments.

title: Seeding ductile nanophase in ceramic grains
abstract: A novel strategy is proposed to achieve coordinated strengthening and toughening of brittle composites by introducing and tailoring ductile nanoparticles within ceramic grains. Transgranular fractures of the ceramic phase were remarkably reduced.

title: Emerging ferroelectric materials ScAlN: applications and prospects in memristors
abstract: The research found that after doping with rare earth elements, a large number of electrons and holes will be produced on the surface of AlN, which makes the material have the characteristics of spontaneous polarization.

title: A UV non-hydrogen pure selenite nonlinear optical material for achieving balanced properties through framework-optimized structural transformation
abstract: NCS NaLu(SeO 3 ) 2 was designed by framework-optimized structural transformation from CS NaGa(SeO 3 ) 2 . NaLu(SeO 3 ) 2 exhibits large SHG responses, a wide band gap, a short UV cut-off edge, high LIDT, sufficient birefringence and high thermal stability.

title: Kinetics vs. thermodynamics: walking on the line for a five-fold increase in MnSi Curie temperature
abstract: Green and digital transitions will induce tremendous demand for metals and semiconductors.

title: Supramolecular control over the variability of color and fluorescence in low-molecular-weight glass
abstract: The color and fluorescence of supramolecular glass were controlled by the non-covalent approach.

title: Photochemical engineering unsaturated Pt islands on supported Pd nanocrystals for a robust pH-universal hydrogen evolution reaction
abstract: Novel core–shell Pd 7 @Pt x heterostructured nanocrystals with Pt-islands shell of unsaturated coordination edges could be directly photodeposited on a reduced graphene oxide support as a highly efficient HER electrocatalyst in a wide pH range.

title: Dual-network DNA–silk fibroin hydrogels with controllable surface rigidity for regulating chondrogenic differentiation
abstract: Dual network DNA-SF hydrogels with controllable surface rigidity for regulating chondrogenic differentiation to repair cartilage defects.

title: Suppressing Jahn–Teller distortion and locking lattice water with doped Fe( iii ) in birnessite toward fast and stable zinc-ion batteries
abstract: A layered Fe–NMO· x H 2 O cathode is fabricated for stable aqueous ZIBs. Fe doping can alleviate the Jahn–Teller effect and lock lattice water, which prevents phase transformation and lattice collapse during the (de)intercalation process.

title: Anode-free post-Li metal batteries
abstract: This comprehensive review explores anode-free post-Li metal batteries (Na, K, Mg, Zn, Al), emphasizing metal-philicity, current collector functionalization strategies, insights from computational studies, and advancements in electrolyte formulations.

title: Multiple free-radical-trapping and hydrogen-bonding-enhanced polyurethane foams with long-lasting flame retardancy, aging resistance, and toughness
abstract: Schematic illustration of the durable flame retardancy, aging resistance, and toughness benefits of hindered amine phosphorus-containing polyol (DTAP) for FPUF.

title: Microdroplet assisted hollow ZnCdS@PDA nanocages’ synergistic confinement effect for promoting photocatalytic H 2 O 2 production
abstract: A ZnCdS@PDA hollow nanoreactor with sulfur vacancies and confinement effects is prepared. The hollow nanoreactor and microdroplets synergistically enrich O 2 for high photocatalytic H 2 O 2 yield under neutral conditions without sacrificial agents.

title: Full-spectrum plasmonic semiconductors for photocatalysis
abstract: This review provides a summary of the characteristics, types, synthesis and characterization of plasmonic semiconductors, the mechanism of full-spectrum plasmonic photocatalysis, and the photocatalytic applications of plasmonic semiconductors.

title: Materials Horizons Emerging Investigator Series: Professor Dr Hai-Bo Zhao, National Engineering Laboratory for Eco-Friendly Polymer Materials (Sichuan), College of Chemistry, Sichuan University
abstract: Our Emerging Investigator Series features exceptional work by early-career researchers working in the field of materials science.

title: Functional polymeric molecules for performing autonomous synthesis of particles with core–shell structures and customizable shapes
abstract: Polymeric molecules in a liquid medium are programmed with instructions written by specific chemical moieties for building structures. The molecules migrate to specific sites for fabricating customized shaped particles with core–shell structures.

title: Biogenic derived nanoparticles modulate mitochondrial function in cardiomyocytes
abstract: PPP NPs revealed multiple functions with identified molecular mechanisms including ROS clearance and m6A modification regulation in alleviating damages and presenting therapeutic roles in mitochondrial and sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy.

title: A transparent p-type semiconductor designed via a polarizability-enhanced strongly correlated insulator oxide matrix
abstract: We report a novel strategy for developing an outstanding transparent p-type conducting oxide exhibiting a deep work function as well as a wide band gap by engineering the polarizability of a strongly correlated NiWO 4 .

title: Correction: Exploring the Mpemba effect: a universal ice pressing enables porous ceramics
abstract: Correction for ‘Exploring the Mpemba effect: a universal ice pressing enables porous ceramics’ by Xiaodan Yang et al. , Mater. Horiz. , 2024, DOI: https://doi.org/10.1039/d3mh01869e.

title: A self-organized sandwich structure of chromium nitride for ultra-long lifetime in liquid sodium
abstract: A novel interface-engineering strategy consisting of the subtle control of the phase transition direction and interface diffusion reaction was proposed, successfully realizing self-organized surfaces and interfaces for use in harsh environments.

title: Synergistic sensitization effects of single-atom gold and cerium dopants on mesoporous SnO 2 nanospheres for enhanced volatile sulfur compound sensing
abstract: A concept of synergistic sensitization effects involving single-atom Au and Ce dopants on mesoporous SnO 2 nanospheres is proposed for ultrasensitive and real-time monitoring of ppb-level volatile sulfur compounds.

title: A high-temperature-triggered crosslinking reaction to achieve excellent intrinsic flame retardancy of organic phase change composites
abstract: A latent crosslinking reaction between the polymer aerogel and organic PCMs was designed, enabling the PCC to form a copolymer monolith with improved charring ability and intrinsic fire safety when overheated, without flame-retardant modification.

title: Thermochemical production of ammonia via a two-step metal nitride cycle – materials screening and the strontium-based system
abstract: Combined theoretical and experimental screening of metal nitrides for the thermochemical production of ammonia without added catalyst.

title: A flexible multimodal pulse sensor for wearable continuous blood pressure monitoring
abstract: We propose a brand-new pressure sensing principle of human-skin’s piezo-thermic transduction, and develop a multisensory pulse sensor, thereby offering a human-skin transducer concept for wearable blood pressure monitoring with generalizability.

title: Highly flexible yet strain-insensitive conjugated polymer
abstract: Freestanding intrinsic conducting polymer films with high and strain-insensitive conductivity, large stretchability, mechanical robustness and healability are produced by coupling poly(3,4-ethylenedioxythiophene) with covalent adaptive networks.

title: Intrinsically thermally conductive polymers
abstract: Polymers are conventionally thought to be thermal insulators, but it is known that some macromolecules host high thermal conductivities. This work reviews the chemostructural features that yield intrinsically thermally conductive polymers.

title: Transition metal-substituted polyoxometalate-ionic liquids with remarkable flame retardancy performance
abstract: Transition-metal-substituted polyoxometalate-based ionic liquids (tmsPOM-ILs) exhibit superior flame retardancy performance owing to their good matrix compatibility and remarkable catalytic charring ability.

title: Injectable extracellular matrix-mimetic hydrogel based on electrospun Janus fibers
abstract: To date, the reported injectable hydrogels have failed to mimic the fibrous architecture of the extracellular matrix (ECM), limiting their biological effects on cell growth and phenotype.

title: Improved capacitive energy storage performance in hybrid films with ultralow aminated molybdenum trioxide integration for high-temperature applications
abstract: The interaction between A-MoO 3 dianhydride monomer of PEI results in the establishment of an intrinsic electric field between the PEI matrix and A-MoO 3 , effectively inhibiting the release of trapped electrons.

title: A lignin-derived carbon dot-upgraded bacterial cellulose membrane as an all-in-one interfacial evaporator for solar-driven water purification
abstract: Solar-driven interfacial evaporation as an efficient approach for wastewater treatment and seawater desalination.

title: Metainterfaces with mechanical, thermal, and active programming properties based on programmable orientation-distributed biometric architectonics
abstract: This study presents next-generation biometric “metainterface” enabling programmable mechanical, thermal, and active interfacial effects for intelligent materials and systems with highly engineerable interfacial properties.

title: High toughness, healable, self-cleaning polydimethylsiloxane elastomers with “rigid-while-flexible” mutual network structure
abstract: The PSiPI elastomer integrates multiple functions and is expected to be applied in outdoor electrical power systems and electronics fields.

title: Bio-inspired facile strategy for programmable osmosis-driven shape-morphing elastomer composite structures
abstract: A polymer composite composed of Ecoflex elastomer and salt particles exhibits extremely large swelling in aqueous solution driven by osmosis and demonstrates its application as active components for various shape-morphing structures.

title: Fully bio-based water-resistant wood coatings derived from tree bark
abstract: A fully bio-based and water-resistant wood surface protection system inspired by the natural barrier function of tree bark.

title: Heterointerface regulation of covalent organic framework-anchored graphene via a solvent-free strategy for high-performance supercapacitor and hybrid capacitive deionization electrodes
abstract: A 2D redox-active pyrazine-based COF was solvent-free anchored on graphene for heterointerface regulation, displaying exciting energy storage and desalination performances.

title: Supramolecular hydrogels for wound repair and hemostasis
abstract: The design and structure of recent supramolecular hydrogels are discussed. The applications of supramolecular hydrogels in hemostasis and wound repair are highlighted. The future development of supramolecular hydrogel dressings is predicted.

title: A highly stable full-polymer electrochemical deionization system: dopant engineering & mechanism study
authors: Louis C. P. M. de Smet
abstract: Dopants with high charge density and structural complexity, combined with balanced operating conditions, can significantly enhance the performance and stability of a full-polymer ECDI system.

title: Materials Horizons Emerging Investigator Series: Dr Mateusz Odziomek, Max Planck Institute of Colloids and Interfaces, Germany
abstract: Our Emerging Investigator Series features exceptional work by early-career researchers working in the field of materials science.

title: A mechanochemical process to capture and separate carbon dioxide from natural gas using boron nitride nanosheets
abstract: A mechanochemical process to store and separate CO 2 gas using boron nitride nanosheets.

title: An evaluation of recent advancements in biological sensory organ-inspired neuromorphically tuned biomimetic devices
abstract: This review unveils advancements in neuroscience, enabling a comprehensive exploration of the capabilities and practical applications of neuromorphic science.

title: Upcycling plastic waste into fully recyclable composites through cold sintering
abstract: Cold sintering enabled the upcycling of polypropylene with gypsum (CaSO 4 ) into a fully recyclable composite, paving the way for the integration of waste into high-performance, recyclable composites.

title: Dual-enzyme decorated semiconducting polymer nanoagents for second near-infrared photoactivatable ferroptosis-immunotherapy
abstract: A dual-enzyme decorated semiconducting polymer nanoagent with second near-infrared photoactivatable therapeutic effects is reported for cancer therapy via enhanced ferroptosis and reversing of the immunosuppressive microenvironment.

title: Aggregation-induced emission organic metal halide complex for X-ray scintillation
abstract: An AIE organic zinc chloride complex scintillator, in which the metal halide serves as X-ray sensitizer for the organic component, is discovered to exhibit a light yield of 13 423 Photon per MeV and a radioluminescence decay lifetime of 5.24 ns.

title: A membrane targeted multifunctional cationic nanoparticle conjugated fusogenic nanoemulsion (CFusoN): induced membrane depolarization and lipid solubilization to accelerate the killing of Staphylococcus aureus
abstract: A cationic metal nanoparticle conjugated fusogenic nanoemulsion (CFusoN) as a lipid solubilizing nanovesicle for the effective treatment of S. aureus infection.

title: Bidirectional optical response hydrogel with adjustable human comfort temperature for smart windows
abstract: A unique bidirectional optical response hydrogel with adjustable temperature for human comfort, remarkable transparency, and solar modulation is developed to contribute to the fields of anti-counterfeiting, information encryption, and smart windows.

title: Self-healing, stretchable and recyclable polyurethane-PEDOT:PSS conductive blends
abstract: Conductive materials obtained from blends of polyurethane, PEDOT:PSS and PEG show exceptional stretchability, toughness, and self-healing properties. Moreover, these materials can be recycled, retaining their mechanical and electrical properties.

title: Composited silk fibroins ensured adhesion stability and magnetic controllability of Fe 3 O 4 -nanoparticle coating on implant for biofilm treatment
abstract: Magnetic propulsion of nano-/micro-robots is an effective way to treat implant-associated infections by physically destroying biofilm structures to enhance antibiotic killing.

title: Enhancement of hybrid organohydrogels by interpenetrating crosslinking strategies for multi-source signal recognition over a wide temperature range
abstract: Enhancement of hybrid organohydrogels by interpenetrating crosslinking strategies for multi-source signal recognition over a wide temperature range.

title: PANDA: a self-driving lab for studying electrodeposited polymer films
abstract: We report the PANDA, a self-driving lab that handles fluids, electrodeposits polymers, and then functionally characterizes the result using optics or electrochemistry. As an example application, we perform a closed-loop study of electrochromic films.

title: Thermochromic hydrogel-based energy efficient smart windows: fabrication, mechanisms, and advancements
abstract: This review provides a comprehensive summary of recent advancements in hydrogel-based thermochromic smart windows, focusing on fabrication, chromic mechanisms, and improvements in responsiveness, stability, and energy-saving performance.

title: Biomimetic growth in polymer gels
abstract: Modeling growth and shape change in polymer gels.

title: High throughput screening of semiconductors with low lattice thermal transport induced by long-range interactions
abstract: Semiconductors with long-range interactions (LRI) due to resonant bonding exhibit delocalized electronic states and low lattice thermal conductivity, contributing to the efficiency of heat-to-electricity conversion.
